authoritative_sources.title: Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. regulator for Magnuson-Moss warranty guidance. This business guide explains warranty and service-contract distinctions, seller and warrantor obligations, dispute posture, and replacement or refund boundaries for consumer products.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: contract grounding, coverage review, and warranty-versus-service boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Service Contracts Model Act
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAIC is the recognized U.S. standards body for model service-contract law used by state regulators. The model act defines service-contract disclosures, free-look cancellation rules, holder duties, prohibited acts, and recordkeeping requirements used to constrain protection-plan workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: contract intake, cancellation handling, holder-duty review, and evidence retention

authoritative_sources.title: Service Contracts, Motor Clubs and Other Extended Warranties Model-Law Chart
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAIC publishes the authoritative state-law chart used to identify where service contracts are regulated under insurance or consumer-protection regimes and where jurisdiction-specific handling changes the safe answer.
used_for: ambiguity recording when state posture differs

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance. The page defines when merchant-side refund or cancellation issues cross into issuer billing-dispute handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch, cancellation refund handling, and customer billing-dispute handoff

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Checklist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. product-safety regulator for many consumer electronics and appliances. The checklist defines stop-sale, inventory isolation, remedy selection, reverse-logistics planning, and escalation ownership for recalled products.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall stop conditions, inventory isolation, and remedy-boundary escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC's handbook is the formal public operating reference for recall remedy packaging. It frames recall remedies as refund, repair, or replacement and pairs them with immediate stop-use messaging that protection-plan execution must not override.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: safety remedy framing and recall communication constraints

workflow_stages.name: Contract intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant, jurisdiction, channel, order, receipt, plan identifier, serial number, contract version, and program context before accepting the work and determine whether the request is in scope or blocked.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Coverage, deductible, and exclusion validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify proof of purchase, enrollment timing, waiting period, covered-failure category, deductible or service fee, prior authorization, exclusion language, and cancellation rights so execution does not proceed on stale or incomplete records.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Repair, replacement, dispatch, or cancellation processing
workflow_stages.description: Review diagnostic notes, authorized-servicer options, BER posture, replacement path, cancellation timing, and refund evidence to determine whether the case can progress to service, replacement, cancellation, or hold.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Payment mismatch and policy-exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Move cancellation refund delays, tender mismatches, deductible-waiver requests, unsupported coverage exceptions, and goodwill pressures into explicit blocked or escalation states rather than forcing completion.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Recall, safety, fraud, and abuse stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve recall, stop-use, inventory-isolation, abuse-indicator, and fraud-sensitive boundaries whenever a protection case carries safety or loss-prevention implications.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when repair, replacement, cancellation, refund, or customer-notice evidence exists and any tenant, jurisdiction, carrier, manufacturer, or program rules stricter than the public baseline have been applied.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: receipts, invoices, order confirmations, plan enrollments, service contracts, and customer account records
claim numbers, authorization notes, diagnostic summaries, dispatch logs, depot-repair tickets, and service-history records
serial numbers, model identifiers, deductible schedules, cancellation requests, refund evidence, and contract-version records
POS transactions, CRM events, contract-administration logs, payment-tender details, refund ledgers, and financing or promotion records
replacement approvals, BER assessments, inventory or replacement-unit states, and vendor or authorized-servicer references
recall notices, stop-use messages, fraud or abuse case references, and escalation notes

allowed: Classify product-protection requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed workflow summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required contract, payment, serial, diagnostic, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, goodwill exception approval, legal interpretation, recall or safety signoff, insurer or manufacturer adjudication, or fraud decisioning.
A public baseline and tenant, jurisdiction, carrier, manufacturer, or program policy materially conflict.
A recall, unsafe condition, suspected abuse, repeated abnormal claim pattern, or customer billing-dispute condition changes whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of repair completion, replacement fulfillment, cancellation processing, refund posting, or customer notification.
Override pricing, deductible, refund, recall, safety, or fraud policies without explicit authority.
Authorize continued use or ordinary protection-plan disposition for recalled or otherwise unsafe products.

domain_failure_modes: the lane approves or denies a protection claim without the controlling contract version or coverage evidence
a cancellation refund is closed to the wrong tender while coverage remains active in the contract system
a deductible or goodwill exception is granted without required approval authority
a recalled product is pushed through ordinary repair or replacement handling without recall ownership
fraud or abuse indicators are treated as ordinary customer-service friction and the claim is completed anyway
jurisdiction-specific service-contract rules are overgeneralized into a confident answer without tenant legal or policy context
electronics and appliance protection work drifts into generic customer support instead of contract, repair, payment, and safety-sensitive execution

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State service-contract treatment, reimbursement-insurance requirements, and administrator-registration rules differ across jurisdictions and programs.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses FTC, NAIC, CFPB, and CPSC sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, tenant program, carrier, administrator, and contract-version facts before applying a final state-specific outcome.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers workflow, evidence, terminology, cancellation and payment handling, jurisdiction variation, and failure behavior instead of only generic warranty policy.

The lane stops at product-protection execution. Final pricing exceptions, legal interpretation, insurer or manufacturer adjudication, fraud findings, and recall or safety signoff remain outside the lane.
